<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data.</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="cnt">Counter to count the number of input data.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="cnt" width_description="[3:0]">Counter to count the number of input data.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <comment>Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit.</comment>
        <case>
            <item>
                <condition>
                    <equal>
                        <input id="rst_n"/>
                        <constant>0</constant>
                    </equal>
                </condition>
                <action>
                    <assign>
                        <reg id="cnt"/> = <constant>0</constant>;
                    </assign>
                    <assign>
                        <output id="dout_valid"/> = <constant>0</constant>;
                    </assign>
                </action>
            </item>
            <item>
                <condition>
                    <and>
                        <equal>
                            <input id="din_valid"/>
                            <constant>1</constant>
                        </equal>
                        <not>
                            <input id="rst_n"/>
                        </not>
                    </and>
                </condition>
                <action>
                    <assign>
                        <reg id="cnt"/> = <reg id="cnt"/> + <constant>1</constant>;
                    </assign>
                    <if>
                        <condition>
                            <equal>
                                <reg id="cnt"/> + <constant>1</constant>
                                <constant>4'b1000</constant>
                            </equal>
                            <action>
                                <assign>
                                    <output id="dout_parallel"/> = <shift>
                                        <input id="dout_parallel"/>,
                                        <input id="din_serial"/>
                                    </shift>;
                                </action>
                            </condition>
                            <else/>
                        </if>
                        <if>
                            <condition>
                                <equal>
                                    <reg id="cnt"/> + <constant>1</constant>
                                    <constant>4'b1001</constant>
                                </equal>
                                <action>
                                    <assign>
                                        <output id="dout_valid"/> = <constant>1</constant>;
                                    </action>
                                </condition>
                            </if>
                        </if>
                    </action>
                </condition>
            </item>
        </case>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers. The inputs are two 8-bit operands. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. Clock. Reset. Signed or unsigned operation. Dividend. Divisor. Valid operation. Result ready for the next operation. Result valid. Result. 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 Next state. Current state. Counter. Negated divisor. Shift register. Subtraction result. Multiplexer select. Carry out.

Design a divider using a sequential logic flow.

On the rising edge of the clock, if the reset signal is high, the module resets all internal registers to zero.

When a valid operation is indicated by opn_valid, the module saves the dividend and divisor, initializes the shift register with the absolute value of the dividend, sets the negative divisor, and initializes the counter.

While the counter is less than 8, the module performs division by attempting to subtract the divisor from the shift register.

If the subtraction is successful, the module updates the shift register with the remainder and increments the counter.

When the counter reaches 8, the module sets the result and indicates that the result is valid.

When the result is ready, the module clears the result and indicates that the result is no longer valid.

samples.xml_description:
<module id="width_8to16">
    <description>Converts 8-bit data input to 16-bit data output.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating the validity of the input data.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating the validity of the output data.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="flag">Flag signal for controlling data output.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="data_lock" width_description="[7:0]">Register for temporarily storing the first valid input data.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="flag">Flag signal for controlling data output.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <when test="&rst_n">
            <block>
                <case test="&valid_in">
                    <statement test="!flag">data_out <= {data_lock, data_in};</statement>
                    <statement test="flag">valid_out <= 1'b1;</statement>
                </case>
                <case test="&valid_in">
                    <statement test="!flag">flag <= 1'b1;</statement>
                    <statement test="flag">flag <= 1'b0;</statement>
                </case>
                <case test="!&valid_in">
                    <statement test="flag">valid_out <= 1'b1;</statement>
                </case>
            </block>
        </when>
        <else>
            <block>
                <case test="!flag">
                    <statement test="!valid_in">data_out <= 16'd0;</statement>
                </case>
                <case test="flag">
                    <statement test="!valid_in">valid_out <= 1'b0;</statement>
                </case>
            </block>
        </else>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="pe">
    <description>Implement a Multiplying Accumulator for 32bit integer.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal. Defined as 1 for reset and 0 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="c" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit output representing the accumulated result.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="MAC_PE">This is a multiplier-accumulator.</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Implement a multiplier-accumulator using a parallel architecture. Use a register to store the partial sum (intermediate accumulation result) and add the product of the input operands to it in each clock cycle.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal traffic light operation, the east-west (EW) cycle is 60 clock cycles of green, 5 clock cycles of yellow, 10 clock cycles of red, and the north-south (NS) cycle is 10 clock cycles of green, 5 clock cycles of yellow, 60 clock cycles of red. The pedestrian button, when pressed, reduces the remaining green time to 10 clocks if the remaining time is greater than 10 clocks. If the remaining green time is less than 10 clocks, the green time is not changed. The lane light and the sidewalk light are paired. When the lane light is green or yellow, the sidewalk light is red. When the lane light is red, the sidewalk light is green.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum name="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum name="s1_red">State 1 red.</enum>
        <enum name="s2_yellow">State 2 yellow.</enum>
        <enum name="s3_green">State 3 green.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic>
            <reg id="cnt" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit counter.</reg>
            <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">2-bit state.</reg>
            <reg id="p_red">1-bit pedestrian red.</reg>
            <reg id="p_yellow">1-bit pedestrian yellow.</reg>
            <reg id="p_green">1-bit pedestrian green.</reg>
            <always>
                <case>
                    <if value="state==idle">Idle state.</if>
                    <if value="state==s1_red">State 1 red.</if>
                    <if value="state==s2_yellow">State 2 yellow.</if>
                    <if value="state==s3_green">State 3 green.</if>
                </case>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic>
            <if value="!rst_n">Reset counter.</if>
            <else>
                <if value="pass_request && green">Reduce green time.</if>
                <else>
                    <if value="!green && p_green">Maintain green time.</if>
                    <else>
                        <if value="!yellow && p_yellow">Maintain yellow time.</if>
                        <else>
                            <if value="!red && p_red">Maintain red time.</if>
                        </else>
                    </else>
                </else>
            </else>
        </sequential_logic>
        <always>
            <assign id="clock">cnt.</assign>
        </always>
        <always>
            <case>
                <if value="!rst_n">Reset outputs.</if>
                <else>
                    <if value="!red && p_red">Set red output.</if>
                    <else>
                        <if value="!yellow && p_yellow">Set yellow output.</if>
                        <else>
                            <if value="!green && p_green">Set green output.</if>
                        </else>
                    </else>
                </else>
            </case>
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <task>
        <if value="!rst_n">Reset task.</if>
        <else>
            <if value="!green && p_green">Maintain green task.</if>
            <else>
                <if value="!yellow && p_yellow">Maintain yellow task.</if>
                <else>
                    <if value="!red && p_red">Maintain red task.</if>
                </else>
            </else>
        </else>
    </task>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Chip clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand (a). Input signal representing the multiplier (b). Product output signal. Chip output flag signal indicating the completion of the multiplication operation. Shift count register, indicating the number of shifts performed in the multiplication operation.

Initialize the shift count register (i) to 0.

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the shift count register (i) is updated.

If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the shift count register (i) is set to 0.

If the shift count register (i) is less than 17 and the start signal is active (start), the shift count register (i) increments by 1.

If the start signal is inactive (!start), the shift count register (i) is reset to 0.

Initialize the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0.

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is updated.

If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is set to 0.

If the shift count register (i) is equal to 16, indicating the completion of the multiplication operation, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is set to 1.

If the shift count register (i) is equal to 17, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is reset to 0.

Initialize the multiplicand register (areg) and multiplier register (breg) to 0.

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs the shift and accumulate operation.

If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the multiplicand register (areg) and multiplier register (breg) are reset to 0.

If the start signal is active (start), the module starts the multiplication operation.

When the shift count register (i) is 0, the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) are stored in the respective registers (areg and breg).

For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high, the product register (yout_r) accumulates the shifted value of the multiplier register (breg) by shifting it left by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.

The product output (yout) is assigned the value of the product register (yout_r).

<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">Set to 8 to represent an 8-bit multiplier.</parameter>
        <parameter id="N_1">Set to 7 to represent the number of bits minus one for an 8-bit multiplier.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_en_out_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the value of mul_en_in for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_en_in"/>
            <output id="mul_en_out_reg"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>On the positive edge of the clock signal.</description>
                    <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                </condition>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Assign the value of mul_en_in to mul_en_out_reg on the positive edge of clk.</description>
                    <left>mul_en_out_reg</left>
                    <right>mul_en_in</right>
                </assignment>
            </always>
            <output id="mul_en_out">
                <description>The output mul_en_out is set to the most significant bit of mul_en_out_reg.</description>
                <connect id="mul_en_out_reg[7]"/>
            </output>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_a_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the value of mul_a for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_a"/>
            <output id="mul_a_reg"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>On the positive edge of the clock signal.</description>
                    <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                </condition>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Assign the value of mul_a to mul_a_reg on the positive edge of clk.</description>
                    <left>mul_a_reg</left>
                    <right>mul_a</right>
                </assignment>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_b_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the value of mul_b for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_b"/>
            <output id="mul_b_reg"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>On the positive edge of the clock signal.</description>
                    <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                </condition>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Assign the value of mul_b to mul_b_reg on the positive edge of clk.</description>
                    <left>mul_b_reg</left>
                    <right>mul_b</right>
                </assignment>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="sum" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Registers to store the intermediate sum values for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="temp"/>
            <output id="sum"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>On the positive edge of the clock signal.</description>
                    <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                </condition>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Assign the value of temp to sum on the positive edge of clk.</description>
                    <left>sum</left>
                    <right>temp</right>
                </assignment>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_out_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the final product value for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_out_reg"/>
            <output id="mul_out"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>On the positive edge of the clock signal.</description>
                    <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                </condition>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Assign the value of adder to mul_out_reg on the positive edge of clk.</description>
                    <left>mul_out_reg</left>
                    <right>adder</right>
                </assignment>
            </always>
            <output id="mul_out">
                <description>The output mul_out is set to the value of mul_out_reg.</description>
                <connect id="mul_out_reg"/>
            </output>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>Reset the registers.</description>
        <if>
            <description>Check if the reset signal is active.</description>
            <condition>
                <description>Reset the registers.</description>
                <not>
                    <connect id="rst_n"/>
                </not>
            </condition>
            <then>
                <description>Reset mul_en_out_reg to 0.</description>
                <assignment>
                    <left>mul_en_out_reg</left>
                    <right>1'b0</right>
                </assignment>
                <description>Reset mul_a_reg to 0.</description>
                <assignment>
                    <left>mul_a_reg</left>
                    <right>8'b0</right>
                </assignment>
                <description>Reset mul_b_reg to 0.</description>
                <assignment>
                    <left>mul_b_reg</left>
                    <right>8'b0</right>
                </assignment>
                <description>Reset sum to 0.</description>
                <assignment>
                    <left>sum</left>
                    <right>8'b0</right>
                </assignment>
                <description>Reset mul_out_reg to 0.</description>
                <assignment>
                    <left>mul_out_reg</left>
                    <right>16'b0</right>
                </assignment>
            </then>
        </if>
        <description>When mul_en_in is active.</description>
        <if>

                <description>mul_en_in is active.</description>

                    </not>
                </and>
            </condition>
            <then>
                <description>Update mul_en_out_reg with the value of mul_en_in.</description>
                <assignment>
                    <left>mul_en_out_reg</left>
                    <right>mul_en_in</right>
                </assignment>
                <description>Update mul_a_reg with the value of mul_a.</description>
                <assignment>
                    <left>mul_a_reg</left>
                    <right>mul_a</right>
                </assignment>
                <description>Update mul_b_reg with the value of mul_b.</description>
                <assignment>
                    <left>mul_b_reg</left>
                    <right>mul_b</right>
                </assignment>
            </then>
        </if>
        <description>Generate partial products based on the bits of mul_b_reg.</description>

            <right>1'b0</right>
        </assign>

                <left>temp[i+1]</left>

<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Implement a module for parallel-to-serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB). The output signal valid_out is set to 1 to indicate the availability of valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the most significant bit of d is output, and the remaining three bits are output sequentially in the following 3 cycles.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial output representing the converted data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="WIDTH" value="4">Width of parallel data input.</param>
        <param id="COUNT" value="3">Count of cycles for parallel-to-serial conversion.</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="cnt" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[2:0]">Counter register for sequential logic.</reg>
        <reg id="valid" type="sequential_logic">Valid register for sequential logic.</reg>
        <reg id="data" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[3:0]">Data register for sequential logic.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential>
            <assign id="cnt[2:0]" next_value="((rst_n) ? cnt[2:0] + 1'b1 : 3'b0)"/>
            <assign id="valid" next_value="(cnt[2:0] == 3'b100 ? 1'b1 : 1'b0)"/>
            <assign id="data[3:0]" next_value="(cnt[2:0] == 3'b100 ? d[3:0] : data[3:0] << 1)"/>
        </sequential>
        <combinational>
            <assign id="dout" value="data[3]"/>
            <assign id="valid_out" value="valid"/>
        </combinational>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.gvd:
module fsm
(
    input IN,
    input CLK,
    input RST,
    output reg MATCH
);

reg [2:0] state, next_state;

parameter S0 = 3'b000, S1 = 3'b001, S2 = 3'b010, S3 = 3'b011, S4 = 3'b100, S5 = 3'b101, S6 = 3'b110, S7 = 3'b111;

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        state <= S0;
        MATCH <= 1'b0;
    end
    else begin
        state <= next_state;
        case (state)
            S0: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            S1: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S2;
                end
            end
            S2: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S3;
                end
            end
            S3: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S4;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            S4: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                    MATCH <= 1'b1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            default: begin
                next_state <= S0;
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Detect a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Support for continuous input and loop detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="S0">Initial state of the FSM.</enum>
        <enum id="S1">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</enum>
        <enum id="S2">State of the FSM when the input is 10.</enum>
        <enum id="S3">State of the FSM when the input is 100.</enum>
        <enum id="S4">State of the FSM when the input is 1001.</enum>
        <enum id="S5">State of the FSM when the input is 10011.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic">Current state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="sequential_logic">Next state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="MATCH" type="sequential_logic">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The FSM detects a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, the output MATCH is 1. The FSM supports continuous input and loop detection. The state transitions are based on the input IN. The next state is determined based on the current state and the value of IN. The output MATCH is set to 1 when the next state is S5 and IN is 1. Otherwise, MATCH is set to 0.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="alu">
    <description>Implement an ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">ALU control signal.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit output representing the result of the operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">A 1-bit output indicating whether a carry occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">A 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">A 1-bit output representing a general flag.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="ADD" value="6'b100000">ADD</enum>
        <enum id="ADDU" value="6'b100001">ADDU</enum>
        <enum id="SUB" value="6'b100010">SUB</enum>
        <enum id="SUBU" value="6'b100011">SUBU</enum>
        <enum id="AND" value="6'b100100">AND</enum>
        <enum id="OR" value="6'b100101">OR</enum>
        <enum id="XOR" value="6'b100110">XOR</enum>
        <enum id="NOR" value="6'b100111">NOR</enum>
        <enum id="SLT" value="6'b101010">SLT</enum>
        <enum id="SLTU" value="6'b101011">SLTU</enum>
        <enum id="SLL" value="6'b000000">SLL</enum>
        <enum id="SRL" value="6'b000010">SRL</enum>
        <enum id="SRA" value="6'b000011">SRA</enum>
        <enum id="SLLV" value="6'b000100">SLLV</enum>
        <enum id="SRLV" value="6'b000110">SRLV</enum>
        <enum id="SRAV" value="6'b000111">SRAV</enum>
        <enum id="LUI" value="6'b001111">LUI</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Uses parameters to define the control signals for various operations, such as ADD, SUB, AND, OR, etc. 
The module assigns the input operands to the signed wires and the output result (r) to the lower 32 bits of the register (res[31:0]). The flag output is determined based on the control signal (aluc) and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise. The zero output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise.
Inside the always block, a case statement is used to perform the appropriate operation based on the control signal (aluc). The result is assigned to the register (res) accordingly. For shift operations (SLL, SRL, SRA, SLLV, SRLV, SRAV), the shift amount is determined by the value of 'a' or 'a[4:0]'. For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of 'a' are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result.
If the control signal (aluc) does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as 'z' (high-impedance).</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a module for edge detection. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). Input signal. Output signal indicating a rising edge. Output signal indicating a falling edge. 0 1 i_state i_next_state The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. It maintains two states:
  • state - the current state of the input signal.
  • next_state - the next state of the input signal.
 On each positive edge of the clock signal clk, it updates the current state i_state to the next state i_next_state. If the reset signal rst_n is low, it resets the state to ZERO. If the current state i_state is ZERO and the input signal a is high, it sets the next state i_next_state to ONE. If the current state i_state is ONE and the input signal a is low, it sets the next state i_next_state back to ZERO. If the input signal a is high, the output signal rise is set to 1. If the input signal a is low, the output signal down is set to 1. Otherwise, both output signals are set to 0. These output signals are synchronized with the clock and remain set to 1 until the corresponding edge appears again.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier. Clock. Active-low reset. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Product. 4 The input signals (mul_a and mul_b) are extended by adding "size" number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.
The module uses a generate block to perform multiplication for each bit position of the multiplier (mul_b) and generate the partial products.
For each bit position i from 0 to size-1, the partial product is calculated as follows:
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 1, the multiplicand is left-shifted by i positions.
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0).
The module uses registers to store the intermediate sum values.
On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs add operations.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the registers are set to 0.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the registers are updated with the sum of the corresponding partial products.
On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module calculates the final product.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the product output (mul_out) is set to 0.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the product output (mul_out) is updated with the sum of registers.

<module id="calendar">
    <description>Implement a perpetual calendar with seconds, minutes, and hours.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK">Clock input.</input>
        <input id="RST">Active high reset signal.</input>
        <output id="Hours" width_description="[5:0]">Current hours.</output>
        <output id="Mins" width_description="[5:0]">Current minutes.</output>
        <output id="Secs" width_description="[5:0]">Current seconds.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="MAX_SECONDS">59</parameter>
        <parameter id="MAX_MINUTES">59</parameter>
        <parameter id="MAX_HOURS">23</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        This module implements a perpetual calendar by continuously updating the seconds, minutes, and hours based on the clock signal and reset signal. 
        The module uses three always blocks to update the values of seconds, minutes, and hours. 
        The first always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge CLK) or the positive edge of the reset signal (posedge RST) to update the seconds value. 
        If the reset signal is active, the seconds value is set to 0. 
        If the seconds value reaches the maximum value (59), it wraps around and is set to 0. 
        Otherwise, it increments the seconds value by 1.
        The second always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal to update the minutes value. 
        If the reset signal is active, the minutes value is set to 0. 
        If both the minutes and seconds values reach the maximum value (59), it wraps around and sets the minutes value to 0. 
        If only the seconds value reaches the maximum, it increments the minutes value by 1. 
        Otherwise, it keeps the minutes value unchanged.
        The third always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal to update the hours value. 
        If the reset signal is active, the hours value is set to 0. 
        If the hours, minutes, and seconds values all reach the maximum value (23, 59, and 59 respectively), it wraps around and sets the hours value to 0. 
        If the minutes and seconds values reach the maximum, it increments the hours value by 1. 
        Otherwise, it keeps the hours value unchanged.
    </implementation>
</module>

module counter_12
(
    input rst_n,
    input clk,
    input valid_count,
    output reg [3:0] out
);
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        out <= 4'b0000;
    end else begin
        if (valid_count && out == 4'd11) begin
            out <= 4'b0000;
        end else if (valid_count) begin
            out <= out + 4'b0001;
        end
    end
end
endmodule

<module id="counter_12">
    <description>Counter from 0 to 11, controlled by valid_count.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low)</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal</input>
        <input id="valid_count">Enable counting</input>
        <output id="out" width_description="[3:0]">Current count</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state">
            <enum_value>Idle=3'b000</enum_value>
            <enum_value>Counting=3'b001</enum_value>
            <enum_value>Done=3'b010</enum_value>
        </enum>

        <sequential_logic>
            <if>
                <condition>Negedge rst_n</condition>
                <true>
                    <assign>out = 4'b0000</assign>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <if>
                        <condition>Posedge clk</condition>

                                <true>
                                    <assign>out = 4'b0000</assign>
                                </true>
                                <false>
                                    <assign>out = out + 4'b0001</assign>
                                </false>
                            </if>
                        </true>
                    </if>
                </false>
            </if>
        </sequential_logic>
    </implementation>

module asyn_fifo is an asynchronous FIFO (First-In-First-Out) buffer with configurable width and depth. It has two separate clock domains for read and write operations, making it asynchronous. The FIFO uses Gray code for its read and write pointers.

The module has the following input and output ports:
- wclk: Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations.
- rclk: Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations.
- wrstn: Write reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.
- rrstn: Read reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.
- winc: Write increment signal. Used to trigger write operations.
- rinc: Read increment signal. Used to trigger read operations.
- wdata: Write data input. The width [WIDTH-1:0] is configurable and represents the data to be written into the FIFO.
- wfull: Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations.
- rempty: Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations.
- rdata: Read data output. The width [WIDTH-1:0] is configurable and represents the data read from the FIFO.

The module uses the following parameters:
- WIDTH = 8: Configurable width of the FIFO data.
- DEPTH = 16: Configurable depth of the FIFO.

The module uses the following internal signals:
- wptr: Write pointer. A 4-bit Gray code counter for the write pointer.
- rptr: Read pointer. A 4-bit Gray code counter for the read pointer.
- wptr_q: Registered write pointer.
- rptr_q: Registered read pointer.
- wfull_r: Internal write full signal.
- rempty_r: Internal read empty signal.

The module uses the following submodules:
- dual_port_ram: A dual-port RAM used for data storage. It has separate write and read ports for the write and read clock domains.

The module's functionality can be described as follows:
- The write pointer (wptr) and read pointer (rptr) are implemented as 4-bit Gray code counters.
- The write and read operations are performed in their respective clock domains, controlled by the write and read increment signals (winc and rinc).
- The dual-port RAM is used for data storage, with separate write and read ports for the write and read clock domains.
- The read and write pointers are synchronized to their respective clock domains using two-stage triggers.
- The write full and read empty signals are generated by comparing the Gray code values of the read and write pointers.

The module's behavior can be further described using state transition graphs and truth tables, but the provided code should be sufficient for a circuit designer to implement the asynchronous FIFO.

```verilog
module asyn_fifo #(
    parameter WIDTH = 8,
    parameter DEPTH = 16
)(
    input wclk,
    input rclk,
    input wrstn,
    input rrstn,
    input winc,
    input rinc,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata,
    output reg wfull,
    output reg rempty,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata
);

localparam ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);

reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wptr, rptr;
reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wptr_q, rptr_q;
wire wfull_r, rempty_r;

dual_port_ram #(
    .WIDTH(WIDTH),
    .DEPTH(DEPTH)
) dual_port_ram_inst (
    .wclk(wclk),
    .wenc(winc),
    .waddr(wptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]),
    .wdata(wdata),
    .rclk(rclk),
    .renc(rinc),
    .raddr(rptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]),
    .rdata(rdata)
);

always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
    if (!wrstn) begin
        wptr <= 0;
    end else if (winc && !wfull_r) begin
        wptr <= wptr + 1;
    end
end

always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
    if (!rrstn) begin
        rptr <= 0;
    end else if (rinc && !rempty_r) begin
        rptr <= rptr + 1;
    end
end

always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
    if (!wrstn) begin
        wptr_q <= 0;
    end else begin
        wptr_q <= wptr;
    end
end

always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
    if (!rrstn) begin
        rptr_q <= 0;
    end else begin
        rptr_q <= rptr;
    end
end

assign wfull_r = (wptr[ADDR_WIDTH] != wptr_q[ADDR_WIDTH]) && (wptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] == rptr_q[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]);
assign rempty_r = (wptr == rptr);

always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
    if (!wrstn) begin
        wfull <= 1'b0;
    end else begin
        wfull <= wfull_r;
    end
end

always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
    if (!rrstn) begin
        rempty <= 1'b0;
    end else begin
        rempty <= rempty_r;
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller.

The method of empty and full judgment is to generate empty and full signals by comparing the Gray code. Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth 8. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number, using the lower three digits of the binary number as the address to access RAM. When the read and write Pointers are equal, the FIFO is null.

When the write pointer has one more cycle RAM than the read pointer, the highest and second-highest bits of the read and write pointer are opposite, the remaining bits are the same, and the FIFO is full. Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations. Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations. Active-low reset signal for both read and write operations. Write increment signal. Triggered when a new data is written into the FIFO. Read increment signal. Triggered when data is read out of the FIFO. Write data input. Data to be written into the FIFO. Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations. Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations. Read data output. Data read out of the FIFO. Width of the FIFO. Depth of the FIFO.

1. Instantiate dual-port RAM module named "dual_port_RAM" and connect it to the FIFO data storage.

2. Implement logic for managing write and read pointers to ensure proper data flow.

3. Use Gray code conversion for write and read pointers to reduce glitches and ensure synchronization.

4. Implement buffer registers for write and read pointers to synchronize the pointers.

5. Determine full and empty conditions based on the comparison of write and read pointers, and control the FIFO operations accordingly.

module signal_generator
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    output reg [4:0] wave
);

reg state;

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (~rst_n) begin
        state <= 0;
        wave <= 0;
    end
    else begin
        case (state)
            0: begin
                if (wave == 31) begin
                    state <= 1;
                end
                else begin
                    wave <= wave + 1;
                end
            end
            1: begin
                if (wave == 0) begin
                    state <= 0;
                end
                else begin
                    wave <= wave - 1;
                end
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule

module JC_counter
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n
);
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        Q <= 64'b0;
    end else begin
        if (Q[0] == 1'b0) begin
            Q <= {Q[63:1], 1'b1};
        end else begin
            Q <= {Q[63:1], 1'b0};
        end
    end
end
endmodule

<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit Johnson counter (torsional ring counter), and the state of the similar 4-bit Johnson counter example is as follows: 0000, 1000, 1100, 1110, 1111, 0111, 0011, 0001, 0000.</description>

        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>

    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="initial_state" value="0">Initial state of the counter.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Reset to 0. Increment when valid_count=1. Wrap at 11.</implementation>
</module>

module RAM
#(
    parameter WIDTH = 6,
    parameter DEPTH = 8
)
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] write_addr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] read_addr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0] read_data
);
    reg [WIDTH-1:0] RAM [DEPTH-1:0];
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            for (int i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
                RAM[i] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
            end
        end else if (write_en) begin
            RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
        end
    end
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
        end else if (read_en) begin
            read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
        end else begin
            read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
        end
    end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. Width of the data. Depth of the RAM.

Define a 8*6 RAM array.

Trigger always block on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

On reset, indicated by !rst_n, all memory locations in the RAM array are cleared to 000000.

Write the data (write_data) to the RAM array at the specified address (write_addr) if the write_en signal is active.

Trigger always block on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

On reset, indicated by !rst_n, the read_data register is cleared to 0.

Read the data at the specified address (read_addr) in the RAM array and assign it to the read_data register if the read_en signal is active.

Read the data at the specified address (read_addr) in the RAM array and assign it to the read_data register if the read_en signal is not active.

Implement a right shifter. Clock. Input signal to be right-shifted. Output signal representing the result of the right shift operation. Width of the input and output signals. sequential_logic The clock signal that triggers updates to the output on each rising edge. The input signal to be shifted to the right. The output signal representing the result of the right shift operation.

Initialize the q register to 0 using the initial statement.

On each rising edge of the clock, right-shift the value of q by 1 bit using the >> operator.

Assign the new input value d to the most significant bit (MSB) of q using the <= operator.

Note that the width of the input and output signals is parameterized by width.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer, data_in will remain constant during the period when data_en is high, and data_en is high for at least 3 clk_b clock cycles. When the value of data_en is high, data can be synchronized. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles. Clock signal A. Clock signal B. Active-low reset signal A. Active-low reset signal B. Input data signal. Input enable signal. Output data signal. Idle state. Wait for enable signal. Synchronize data. Register to store input data. Register to store enable data. Enable control register. Enable control register. The module includes a register, data_reg, to store the input data signal, data_in.
On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the register (data_reg) is set to 0.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (data_reg) is updated with the input data signal (data_in).
The module includes a register, en_data_reg, to store the input enable signal, data_en.
On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the register (en_data_reg) is set to 0.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (en_data_reg) is updated with the input enable signal (data_en).
The module includes two registers, en_clap_one and en_clap_two, to control the selection of the input data.
On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module updates the registers.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, both registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are set to 0.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is high, the registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are updated based on the value of en_data_reg.
The register en_clap_one is assigned the value of en_data_reg.
The register en_clap_two is assigned the previous value of en_clap_one.
On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module assigns the output data value.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the output data (dataout) is set to 0.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is high and the control signal (en_clap_two) is active, the output data (dataout) is assigned the value of the data register (data_reg).
If the control signal (en_clap_two) is inactive, the output data (dataout) retains its previous value.
